_note: Deng Ai's five-reason analysis correctly predicts Jiang Wei's return; defeat at Duan Gu (256); Zhuge Dan launches his Shouchun rebellion (May 257).
Siege of Shouchun; Yu Quan's loyal death; Jiang Wei's parallel raid on Changcheng. Spans 257 through Zhuge Dan's fall (February 258).
Ding Feng plots and kills Sun Chen on Sun Xiu's orders (December 258); Sun Liang deposed earlier that autumn; Jiang Wei's array repulses Deng Ai temporarily.
Cao Mao's failed coup against Sima Zhao — killed at the South Gate (June 260); Jiang Wei loses his supply train and the Plank Trail.

Text explicitly cites 'fifth year of Wonderful Sight, in Shu-Han calendar (AD 262)' for Jiang Wei's farm-camp retreat; Sima Zhao orders Zhong Hui's invasion (third year of Jingyuan ≈ 262, march in 263).
Zhong Hui's army divides into the three Hanzhong valleys; Zhuge Liang's apparition at Dingjun Mountain. Autumn 263.
Deng Ai's audacious crossing of the Yinping Mountains via roped descent; Zhuge Zhan and Zhuge Shang die defending Mianzhu (November 263).
Liu Chen (Prince of Beidi) suicides at the Zhaolie Temple; the Latter Ruler surrenders at Chengdu (November 263); Sima Zhao plans the western expedition to deal with Zhong Hui.
Zhong Hui and Jiang Wei's joint mutiny + deaths (January 264); the Latter Ruler's 'I am happy here' anecdote in Luoyang; Sima Yan accepts Cao Huan's abdication and founds Jin (February 266).
Sun Xiu dies and Sun Hao enthroned (text explicit 'first year of Prosperous Beginning (AD 264)'); Du Yu and Wang Jun's joint river-and-land conquest of Wu reunites the empire (March 280).
